name: task_overdue_3_days
description: Active task, due 3 days ago, has prior context in notes
criteria: Response indicates the task is overdue by about 3 days
Response suggests prioritizing or completing the task
Response is under 80 words total
Response does not restate the title 'Reply to Sarah's proposal' as a sentence

name: task_stale_12_days
description: Active task, created 12 days ago, no due date, no notes
inputData:
Item type: task
Title: Research new CRM options
Status: active
Created: {{today-12d}}
Due: none
Notes: (empty)
criteria: Response flags the task as stale or sitting without updates
Response references that it has been sitting for around 12 days
Response suggests either doing it or removing/archiving it
Response is under 100 words total

name: task_due_tomorrow
description: Active task, due in 1 day, has meaningful notes
criteria: Response notes the urgency — due within a day or tomorrow
Response is under 80 words total
Response does not restate the title 'Finalize board deck' verbatim as a sentence

name: task_normal_with_notes
description: Active task, due in 25 days, notes describe venue and attendees
criteria: Response suggests a concrete, specific next step grounded in the notes (e.g., book venue, survey team for dates, compare cities)
Response is under 80 words total
Response does not restate the title 'Plan Q3 offsite' as a sentence

name: task_multistep_due_soon
description: Active complex task, due in 3 days, notes list multiple workstreams
criteria: Response acknowledges the multi-step nature of the migration
Response suggests starting with a specific piece named in the notes (schema migration, data backfill, dashboard updates, or team training)
Response references the 3-day timeline or urgency
Response is under 80 words total

name: task_empty_notes_interview
description: Active task, due in 3 days, empty notes, title references an interview
criteria: Response suggests a concrete next step (review resume, prep questions, or check calendar)
Response is under 80 words total
Response does not invent details not in the input (no fabricated candidate name, no fabricated company)

name: event_small_1on1_soon
description: Calendar event, starts in 4 hours, 1 other attendee, short description
inputData:
Item type: calendar event
Title: 1:1 with Jordan
Start: today at 2:30 PM (in about 4 hours)
Attendees: Jordan Chen
Description: weekly check-in
criteria: Response correctly identifies it as a 1:1, small meeting, or check-in
Response is under 80 words total
Response does not fabricate agenda items beyond 'weekly check-in'

name: event_large_tomorrow
description: Calendar event tomorrow, 6 attendees, description names Q3 planning and OKRs
inputData:
Item type: calendar event
Title: Q3 Planning Kickoff
Start: tomorrow at 10:00 AM
Attendees: Jordan Chen, Lena Park, Marcus Chen, Alex Rivera, Sarah Kim, Dan Cole
Description: Review OKRs and discuss team priorities for Q3.
criteria: Response notes this is a larger meeting OR references the 6-person attendee count
Response references Q3 planning or OKRs
Response is under 80 words total

name: event_imminent_with_agenda
description: Calendar event starts in about 90 minutes, has a linked agenda doc, 2 external attendees
inputData:
Item type: calendar event
Title: Customer QBR with Acme Corp
Start: today at 11:00 AM (in about 90 minutes)
Attendees: Alexandra Liu, Tom Patel
Description: Review Q1 usage metrics. Agenda doc: https://docs.google.com/document/d/abc123. Discuss renewal.
criteria: Response references prep work or the linked agenda doc
Response notes the imminent timing (within 2 hours, very soon, or similar)
Response is under 80 words total

name: event_interview_tomorrow
description: Calendar event tomorrow, product sense round, resume attached

Title: Interview — Senior PM candidate Alex Rivera

Attendees: Alex Rivera, Jordan Chen

name: content_investor_notes
description: Saved content item with the user's own notes on a specific company's investor thesis
inputData:
Item type: content
Title: Function Health investor thesis
Source: internal notes
Saved: {{today-10d}}
Notes: Strike price, cap table, risks, and bull case.
criteria: Response gives a concrete reason revisiting the notes might be useful
Response references at least one element from the notes (thesis, cap table, risks, or bull case)
Response is under 80 words total

name: task_completed_recently
description: Completed task, finished today, has a note about where announcement posted
criteria: Response does not urge the user to do the task — it is already completed
Response either acknowledges it is done OR makes a brief observation about the shipped work
Response is under 60 words total

name: task_no_due_date_today
description: Active task created today, no due date, notes describe a trend
inputData:
Item type: task
Title: Explore Snowflake cost optimization
Status: active
Created: {{today}}
Due: none
Notes: Current spend trending up 20% MoM.

Response does not state a specific invented deadline as if it were a fact (suggesting the user set a deadline is fine; fabricating 'Due: Friday' is not)
Response is under 100 words total

name: event_mid_meeting
description: Calendar event happening right now (user opens take while in the meeting), 4 attendees, has a description
inputData:
Item type: calendar event
Title: Weekly Product Sync
Start: started 15 minutes ago (in progress now)
Attendees: Jordan Chen, Lena Park, Marcus Chen, Alex Rivera
Description: Review user research insights from the new navigation test.
criteria: Response acknowledges the meeting is currently in progress OR offers something useful to the user right now (e.g., reference the user research topic)
Response does not suggest preparing for the meeting (it is already happening)
Response is under 60 words total
